notes: French micro-brand based in Paris. Founded 2019. Retro-inspired designs with strong vintage aesthetic. Swiss-assembled. Visitability unconfirmed.

Hungarian independent watchmaker and AHCI member from a three-generation watchmaking family. Hand-makes every component of his wristwatches — cases, bridges, dials, hands, and buckles — in his Budapest atelier.
French independent with nautical and sailing heritage. Known for robust field and maritime watches. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand specializing in vintage-inspired watches with a focus on affordable, quality timepieces. Known for their retro design aesthetic and attention to detail.
Croatian watch manufacturer with a long heritage. Produces a range of watches from dress to sports models.
Swiss independent from Les Brenets near Le Locle. Known for skeleton pocket watch-inspired designs. Family-owned. Visitability unconfirmed.
Icelandic microbrand known for minimalist Scandinavian-designed watches with Miyota quartz and automatic movements.
French independent with military and aviation heritage. Produces robust field watches. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swedish independent watchmaker known for vintage-inspired military and field watches with ETA movements and hand-finished details.
Paris-based French independent. Swiss-assembled watches with clean, minimalist design language. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.

Alain Silberstein is a Paris-based French independent watchmaker internationally recognised for Bauhaus-influenced designs using bold primary colours and geometric forms, with collaborations spanning Blancpain, MB&F, and other major Swiss manufactures. His Paris atelier is the creative origin of all his work, though movement production is handled in Switzerland. A visit would engage with his design archives and distinctive artistic philosophy. Visitability would need to be arranged directly given his individual-atelier model.
German independent watchmaker specializing in pilot and mechanical watches with in-house designs, operating since the 1990s.
French micro-brand based in Paris. Colourful, design-led watches aimed at the contemporary French market. Visitability unconfirmed.
German microbrand creating hand-assembled watches with a focus on quality and minimalist design.
Italian microbrand founded in 1997. Known for vintage-inspired military and diving watches with robust tool-watch aesthetics. Produces automatic watches with accessible pricing.
Known for exceptional grand feu enamel dials made in-house — one of very few contemporary watchmakers creating enamel dials. Founded by Lewis Heath. Visits by arrangement.

Paris-based French independent. Known for considered, stripped-back dial design and high-quality Swiss movements. Part of the new wave of French micro-brands emphasising design discipline over decoration. Visitability unconfirmed.
German diving watch manufacturer with heritage in professional dive watch production. Known for robust tool watches.
Swiss microbrand known for affordable dive watches and vintage-style timepieces. Offers quartz and automatic movements at entry-level prices.
French independent inspired by the North Wind (Aquilon) and sailing. Nautical and adventure watch aesthetic. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
Pan-Scandinavian brand founded in Copenhagen (Frederiksberg) using mostly Scandinavian materials and components.
German microbrand based in Glashutte. Known for classic Bauhaus-influenced tool watches using in-house calibres and traditional watchmaking methods.
German watch brand with heritage in aviation watches. Produces quartz and automatic sports watches with military aesthetic and accessible pricing.
Independent Swiss watchmaker based in Tramelan. Known for mechanical watches blending traditional craftsmanship with contemporary design; family-owned since 1875.
Berlin-based brand reviving the historic Askania instrument and watch name. Confirm current production status and visitability.

Independent watchmaking atelier in Besançon — France's historic horological capital. Produces hand-finished, bespoke watches drawing on traditional Franc-Comtois techniques. Visitability potentially by arrangement — confirm before visiting.
German watch brand with heritage dating back to 1950s. Known for classic automatic watches and sports models with reliable movements at mid-range prices.

French independent watch brand based in Paris offering contemporary design with a deliberate French identity. Micro-brand scale targeting collectors seeking alternatives to Swiss-dominated luxury. Showroom or atelier visits not formally advertised; direct contact needed to assess whether a luxury group experience is feasible.
Historic Le Locle brand, founded 1898. Specialises in themed and artistic dials. Visitability unconfirmed.
Romanian independent watchmaker specializing in hand-assembled mechanical watches with classical styling and bespoke options.

A historic Besançon brand with a distinguished history supplying flyback chronographs to the French military, including the Armée de l'Air — one of few brands with continuous French military supply heritage alongside Dodane. Revived with a focus on French aviation timing heritage. Current production location and visitor access should be confirmed directly; an excellent complement to a Besançon horological itinerary.
British brand specializing in affordable aviation-themed watches inspired by vintage military pilot chronographs. Known for quartz and automatic sports watches.
Sustainable Swiss independent based in Morges. Produces watches from recycled ocean plastic cases and titanium. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss micro-brand with distinctive design aesthetic. Based in La Chaux-de-Fonds. Visitability unconfirmed.
Independent Danish watchmaker Rune Hjorth Bakkendorff handcrafts small-series and unique wristwatches and clocks in his workshop near Copenhagen in Charlottenlund.
Ballast is a German watch brand known for pilot watches and field watches with strong Bauhaus design influences.

French micro-brand based in Paris. Known for clean, restrained dial design and quality Swiss movements at accessible price points. Aimed at the contemporary French independent watch community. Visitability unconfirmed.

Baltic is a Paris-based French independent brand founded in 2017 by Etienne Malec, producing vintage-inspired mechanical watches at enthusiast-accessible price points. Design and brand direction originate entirely from Paris, with manufacturing partnerships in France and Switzerland. The brand operates a Paris studio used for trade, press, and enthusiast visits by arrangement — a representative visit for the contemporary Paris independent watchmaking scene.
Balticus is a Lithuanian independent watchmaker known for Baltic-inspired designs and mechanical watches with cultural significance.
London-based customizer and watch brand known for bespoke dial designs and vintage watch restorations. Specializes in personalized timepieces with custom aesthetics.
Dutch microbrand based in Amsterdam.
Batiscafo is an Italian dive watch brand known for professional-grade underwater watches and vintage-inspired designs.
Bausele is a German microbrand producing minimalist tool watches and field watches with Bauhaus design philosophy.
Paris-based French independent. Colourful, hand-assembled watches with artisan focus. Visitability unconfirmed.
Hamburg-based independent. Architecture-inspired design language. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss independent based in the Basel region. Small-series watches with clean, understated design. Visitability unconfirmed.
Award-winning British designer creating illuminated luxury art timepieces (Tempus series) featuring LED-illuminated mechanical movements. Australian-born, UK-based studio in London.
Swiss independent brand headquartered in Neuchatel producing Swiss-made tonneau tourbillons with French and Italian aesthetic influences.
Slovak independent watchmaker producing mechanical watches with focus on mechanical quality and design.
Swiss independent based in Geneva. Known for stealthy all-black treated watches and stealth-spec military aesthetic. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watchmaker known for bold, distinctive designs and skeleton watches. Combines technical innovation with unconventional aesthetics.
German microbrand known for innovative and distinctive watch designs, including the famous One watch with unique 24-hour dial.
Design-focused German brand in Öhringen, Baden-Württemberg. Known for minimalist single-hand watches. Confirm visitability.
Small Stockholm-based Swedish independent watchmaker. Confirm current active status and visitability.
German microbrand producing mechanical watches with in-house designs. Known for tool watches and aviation-inspired timepieces.
British independent watchmaker founded in 2002. Known for aviation-inspired watches with British heritage and chronometer-grade movements.
Founded in 2010 by Vincent Dupontreue. Swiss independent brand known for innovative complications including mechanical altimeter mechanisms.
French brand founded 2013. Assembly and finishing in Charquemont, Doubs. Swiss movements. EUR 150–600. Verify whether a facility visit is viable for a luxury group — scale may be limited.
French watchmaker known for automotive-inspired and motorsport-themed timepieces with distinctive dial designs.

Atelier near Bordeaux (Gironde). Motorsport and aviation-inspired watches with movements and cases made in-house in France including titanium and carbon fibre. High vertical integration for a French brand. EUR 5,000–30,000+.
Paris-based French independent named after the historic Rue de Buci on the Left Bank. Art-inspired dials with Parisian cultural references. Swiss-assembled. Visitability unconfirmed.
Brand of GFF Suisse SA based in Mendrisio, Ticino. Created to honour diving medicine pioneer Dr. Albert Buehlmann by integrating his decompression algorithms into purpose-built dive watches.

Geneva-based independent brand known for the Winch Tourbillon, which uses a horizontal cylindrical barrel driven by a chain-and-fusée mechanism visible through the dial — a world first at its 2007 launch. Production is limited to around 10 pieces per year. Atelier visit information for groups has not been publicly confirmed.
A Swiss watch brand with a history in watchmaking, though details about current operations are limited.
French independent watchmaker known for classical dress watches with Art Deco influences. Small-scale production focusing on traditional watchmaking techniques.
Swiss watch brand that produced affordable watches. Part of the Technos Group, now primarily known as a value-oriented timepiece manufacturer.
Parisian independent known for bold colours, contemporary design, and Swiss movements. Strong French identity. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.

Founded in 1974, CVDK is the Netherlands' longest-established independent watchmaking atelier, internationally recognised for astronomical complications including planetarium displays, orreries, and astronomical calendars. The atelier is in Franeker, Friesland — home to the Eise Eisinga Planetarium (1781), the world's oldest working planetarium, creating a compelling combined itinerary of watchmaking and Dutch scientific heritage. Production is on a bespoke and very limited-edition basis. Group visits require prior arrangement; the dual draw of the watchmaker and the Eisinga Planetarium makes Franeker a distinctive luxury travel destination.
British microbrand founded in 2004. Known for affordable mechanical watches with in-house design and quality finishing, using ETA and Sellita movements. Price range £600–£1,500.
French independent based in Paris. Sports and field watch aesthetic with robust construction. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss independent watch brand founded in 1983. Specializes in mechanical watches with distinctive design and in-house movements. Known for avant-garde styling and high-quality finishing.
Swiss brand based in Biel/Bienne. Heritage brand with Art Deco styling. Visitability unconfirmed.
German microbrand founded in 2017. Known for minimalist Bauhaus-inspired dive and dress watches using ETA and Ronda movements. Sustainable production focused.
Disruptive Swiss independent based in Geneva. Direct-to-consumer, transparent pricing model. Community-driven design process. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss luxury watch manufacturer with a long heritage. Known for dress watches and precision timepieces with distinctive design language.
Swiss microbrand specializing in affordable mechanical watches with modern and vintage-inspired designs.
British microbrand focused on elegant dress watches and vintage-inspired timepieces with accessible pricing.
German microbrand known for sporty chronographs and avant-garde designs with in-house movements and complications.
Swiss microbrand founded in 2007, specializes in diving watches and chronographs with Swiss-made movements.
Independent Swiss watchmaker founded 2016, creates classically-inspired dress watches and sports watches with in-house design.
Dutch independent watchmaker focused on mechanical watches and vintage restoration.
Milanese independent known for ultra-thin, colourful, design-led watches. Swiss-assembled. Strong Italian fashion-adjacent positioning. Visitability unconfirmed.
German microbrand founded 1994, known for tool watches, dive watches, and chronographs with German-made cases and movements.
Danish watch brand known for minimalist Scandinavian-style dress and casual watches.
Swiss watch manufacturer founded 1993, produces sports and dive watches at accessible price points with Swiss movements.
Berlin independent known for minimal, functional design. Confirm visitability.
Swiss watch brand producing sports and dress watches at mid-range pricing.
Dutch independent watchmaker creating bespoke and limited-edition mechanical watches.
Swiss dive watch specialist based in Lengnau. Established 1924. Known for the Santiago and Shell Star divers. Visitability unconfirmed.
Historic British case maker and watchmaker, known for vintage watch cases and restoration work.
French independent with aviation and motorsport heritage. Produced in collaboration with Swiss manufacturers. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
German watch brand known for sporty designs and affordable mechanical watches.
German microbrand reviving historical timepiece designs with modern manufacturing.
German independent watchmaker focusing on vintage-inspired, hand-assembled mechanical watches. Known for classical designs and quality manufacturing.
Lithuanian independent microbrand creating vintage-inspired watches with accessible pricing.
French independent microbrand creating minimalist and field watches with vintage aesthetics.
German microbrand reviving historical timepiece designs with modern manufacturing.

Specialist aviation and military watch manufacturer established 1857 in Besançon, the historic French watchmaking capital. Supplies French Air Force and other military clients. Small-scale bespoke production, EUR 800–4,000.
German independent watchmaker known for precision mechanical watches and hand-finishing craftsmanship.
Swiss diving watch brand founded in 1889, known for distinctive orange dial professional dive watches and vintage chronographs. Uses ETA/Sellita and in-house movements.
A British watch brand known for producing affordable mechanical watches with classical designs.
British heritage-inspired microbrand known for retro-styled dress and pilot watches. Uses reliable mechanical movements at accessible price points.
One of Switzerland's oldest watchmaking families, re-established in Le Locle. Heritage complications and artistic dial work. Visitability unconfirmed.
German microbrand known for Bauhaus-inspired minimalist design and affordable mechanical watches. Uses ETA and Ronda movements.
A Swiss independent watchmaker creating luxury mechanical watches.
British watch brand created in 2012, named after legendary horologist John Harrison. Known for classic dress watches and skeleton dial pieces at mid-range prices.
Swiss watchmaker founded in 1887, known for vintage chronographs and dress watches. Uses ETA and Swiss movements, repositioned as independent microbrand.
Italian independent based in Milan. Known for minimalist, Bauhaus-influenced watch design with strong Italian aesthetic. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss microbrand founded in 1884, known for chronographs and sports watches. Uses ETA movements, positioned as independent brand under $2,000.
French independent brand with vintage-inspired identity rooted in mid-century French watchmaking heritage. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand creating rugged, military-inspired dive and field watches. Known for British design and robust construction under $1,500.
German watch brand known for affordable mechanical watches and dress pieces with ETA movements.
Vintage Swiss watch brand founded in 1913, known for Sherpa mountaineering watches and chronographs. Repositioned as modern microbrand.
Swiss watchmaker founded in 1949, known for dress watches and chronographs with mechanical movements. Uses ETA and in-house calibers.

Historic Swiss brand based in La Chaux-de-Fonds. Founded in the early 20th century. Known for the integrated bracelet construction — the Eska bracelet was a significant technical achievement. Revived and continuing the tradition. Visitability unconfirmed.
London-based British independent. Known for bold colours, travel-time complications, and vintage GMT references. Swiss-assembled. Visitability unconfirmed.
Revival of a historic Bristol watch company by the original founders' descendants. Collaborated with Garrick on a handmade movement watch. Confirm current visitability.
Swiss independent based in Baar, Zug. Known for patented floating case suspension system on the Essence. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watch manufacturer based in the Valais region, known for professional watches including pilot and diving models. Independent brand using ETA and in-house movements.
Swiss AHCI member working from a rural atelier near Bern in Wichtrach. Known for mysterious and tourbillon complications in both table clocks and wristwatches.
Geneva-based independent founded by Arnaud Furlan and Fabrice Marri. Known for vintage-inspired dress watches and two-tone dials. Visitability unconfirmed.

Italian independent watch brand based in Florence (GaGà Milano parent company). Design-forward Italian watchmaking with bold aesthetic and distinctive large-format case designs. Visitability unconfirmed.
Handcrafted British watches built to order in their Norfolk workshop. Components manufactured in Norwich. Small workshop, visits by arrangement.
British microbrand known for affordable dive watches and tool watches with Japanese movements. Based in the UK with a focus on practical, no-nonsense timepieces.
Swiss microbrand producing affordable automatic and quartz watches with modern designs. Known for good value proposition in entry-level mechanical watches.
Swiss microbrand founded in 1953, known for dive watches and the iconic Combat Sub. Recently revived as an independent brand producing reliable automatic timepieces.
British luxury watch brand founded in 1695, known for chronographs and mechanical watches. Produces complications and sports watches with in-house movements.
German watchmaker and microbrand known for precision instruments and tool watches with high-quality finishing and German engineering.

Parisian French independent named as a nod to the great French artisans. Known for clean, vintage-inflected designs and Swiss movements with a strong emphasis on French craftsmanship values. Visitability unconfirmed.
Independent Swiss manufacture founded in 1828, known for haute horlogerie timepieces and distinctive in-house movements. Premium positioning with prices above $5,000 but maintains independence.

Maria and Richard Habring's small atelier in Völkermarkt, Carinthia, Austria. One of the smallest true manufactures — produces around 200 watches per year using repurposed and re-engineered historical ébauches. Exceptional value at this craft level. Personal atelier visits possible by arrangement.

Halda revives Sweden's most historically grounded watch name, rooted in the Svängsta factory established in the 1870s and known for precision instruments and early wristwatches sold across Europe and North America. The modern brand relaunched around 2017–2019 in Gothenburg, producing mechanical watches that reference this Swedish industrial heritage. Visitability of the studio is not publicly documented; access for a luxury group would need to be arranged directly with the brand.
German chronograph manufacturer founded in 1882. Known for aviation and racing chronographs with mechanical movements and distinctive design.
Small independent brand from Haute-Rive, canton of Neuchâtel. Visitability unconfirmed.
Paris-based French independent. Known for modular, customisable watch concept. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand specializing in dive watches with in-house designed cases. Known for robust tool watches built to professional standards.
One-man German watchmaking brand founded in Munich by a graduate of Germany's oldest watchmaking school and former Patek Philippe Munich employee. Produces handmade watches entirely in Munich.
French independent based in Paris. Produces understated tool and field watches with a strong focus on legibility and functional design. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss manufacture founded in 2007 by Andreas Felsl and Tzuyu Huang. Headquartered in Biel/Bienne producing watches with exceptionally high Swiss content (87-98%).
Machiel Hulsman founded Hulsman Timepieces in 2014 in Heerde, Gelderland. Credited with creating the first fully hand-made Dutch wristwatch.
Swiss independent watchmaker known for classical dress watches with traditional finishing and in-house movements.
Geneva-based sustainable independent. Watches made from upcycled Swiss franc coins. B-Corp certified. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss independent watch brand registered in Geneva. Originally co-designed with Australian designer Marc Newson; known for iconic pod-shaped cases.
British microbrand known for vintage-inspired tool watches and military-style timepieces with robust construction.
German microbrand specializing in vintage-inspired Bauhaus-style watches, known for precision and retro aesthetics with mechanical movements.
London-based independent with strong community following. Known for the Aquamatic diver and collaboration releases. Visitability unconfirmed.
Danish design-focused watch brand known for minimalist Scandinavian aesthetics and modern styling.
Paris-based French independent. Known for hand-finished dress watches with traditional French aesthetic and enamel dial work. Small atelier production. Visitability unconfirmed.
Independent British watchmaker trained at the British Horological Institute. Began producing watches in 2020. Known for handmade silver cases with champlevé enamel dials.
Historic British watchmaker, originally established as a London-based pocket watch maker.
Swiss independent watchmaker known for elegant dress watches and complications with in-house movements.
Swiss heritage brand founded in 1681, known for chronographs and mechanical watches with Swiss movements.
British watch brand specializing in military-inspired designs with emphasis on durability and readability.
German watch manufacturer founded in 1861, known for precision timekeepers, quartz and automatic watches, and iconic Bauhaus design.
German watch brand inspired by aviation heritage, known for robust tool watches and German engineering with mechanical movements.
French microbrand known for affordable, colorful sports and casual watches with accessible pricing.
Founded in 2022 by two former A. Lange & Sohne watchmakers, Johannes Kallinich and Thibault Claeys. Represents the next generation of Saxon independent watchmaking in Glashutte.
Swedish microbrand known for minimalist Scandinavian-design watches with clean dials and accessible pricing.
German microbrand watch manufacturer located in Kiel, known for high-quality German-made timepieces.

Historic French watch brand originally founded in the 1950s as a Timex subsidiary. Revived as an independent French brand. Known for accessible, design-led French watches. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
German watch brand known for crafted mechanical watches.
Dutch independent watchmaker crafting handmade mechanical watches with emphasis on quality movements and artisanal production.
Dutch independent brand founded in 2009, known for innovative modular watch designs with interchangeable cases and straps.
Swedish microbrand specializing in field watches and minimalist designs with Scandinavian aesthetics.
German microbrand producing vintage-inspired field and dive watches with German craftsmanship and accessible price points.
Danish microbrand focused on minimalist watch design and quality construction.
Geneva-based design-led independent. Known for the Tourbillon watch-speaker fusion device. Luxury lifestyle positioning. Visitability unconfirmed.

Historic clock and table clock manufacturer in Sainte-Suzanne, Jura. Founded 1839. Now part of MB&F's extended family. Produces highly complex automata, table clocks and a small number of wristwatches. Visits by arrangement.
Founded in 2011 by Emmanuel Curti in Geneva. Specialises in the bespoke customisation of iconic models from major Swiss houses, applying unique materials and finishes.
German watchmaker with heritage dating to WWII, known for Flieger-style pilot watches with Miyota or ETA movements and vintage aesthetics.
German independent watchmaker known for handcrafted mechanical watches with in-house finishing and traditional horological approach.
Swedish microbrand producing minimalist Scandinavian watches with focus on clean design and accessible pricing.
Paris-based French independent with motorsport-inspired design. Swiss movements. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.

French independent brand founded in Toulouse, producing watches that incorporate materials from French aerospace heritage including certified components from Concorde and Airbus aircraft. Assembly and finishing carried out in France — one of very few genuinely made-in-France independent ateliers. Atelier visits for small luxury groups can be arranged directly; compelling for groups interested in the intersection of French watchmaking and aerospace.

French independent based in Paris. 'Le Garde Temps' (the timekeeper) reflects a traditional French watchmaking identity. Known for classic dial layouts and hand-finished details. Visitability unconfirmed.
Small Swiss independent based in La Chaux-de-Fonds. Visitability unconfirmed.
German microbrand founded 2012, specializing in minimalist urban watches with Bauhaus-influenced design and accessible price range.
Danish-Swiss independent brand founded 2002, known for sport watches with innovative modular designs and use of quality movements.
Historic French watchmaker with heritage back to 1867, currently producing affordable vintage-inspired watches and sports models.

Locman is a distinctively Italian independent brand founded in 1986 and headquartered on the Island of Elba, Tuscany — a remote and historically resonant location famous as the site of Napoleon's exile. The brand is known for bold, design-forward watches using unconventional materials including titanium, carbon fibre, and aluminium. The Elba location creates a compelling travel proposition: a watchmaking atelier visit combined with an Italian island itinerary is highly unusual on the European luxury circuit. Group visitability is unconfirmed and would require direct arrangement.
Italian independent watchmaker founded 1974, known for luxury sport watches with Italian design aesthetic and ceramic/titanium cases.

French independent based in Paris. Named as a cultural homage in the French tradition. Produces clean, contemporary watches with a minimalist French design ethos. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss independent manufacture founded 1974, known for classic dress watches and chronographs with in-house designed movements and traditional aesthetics.

Czech Republic's first and only AHCI member, based in Mohelnice. Debuted the Karel Rotation at Baselworld 2019 — a wristwatch with a radically original rotating-base movement featuring two independent movements sharing one spring barrel.

Geneva-based independent concept brand created by Maximilian Büsser (founder of MB&F). M.A.D. Edition produces accessible mechanical watches using unconventional and playful design language — distinct brand identity and separate from MB&F. Based in Geneva. Visitability unconfirmed.
Dutch microbrand founded in 2015 known for minimalist, modernist watch designs with emphasis on clean aesthetics and technical quality. Produces watches with Swiss and Japanese movements.
Paris-based French independent. Produces jewellery-inspired watches with artistic, feminine design language. Small-series and bespoke pieces. Visitability unconfirmed.
Parisian independent watch house. Bespoke and small-series watches with artisanal French craftsmanship. Visitability unconfirmed.
British watch brand based in Manchester known for crafting vintage-inspired mechanical watches. Emphasis on British design heritage and independent watchmaking.
German watch brand known for producing homage watches and custom designs. They manufacture watches with various movement options and focus on quality at accessible price points.
Italian independent watchmaker Marco Guarino from Chieri near Turin. Works from a workshop in the hills near Asti, Piedmont. Specialises in astronomical complications.
Paris-based French independent with boutique on the Left Bank. Swiss-assembled, strongly French aesthetic. Design-led with focus on vintage-inspired and artistic watches. Visitability unconfirmed.
Independent watchmaker based in Glashütte. Trained in the Glashütte tradition. Known for clean, architecturally pure dial design with in-house finishing. Visits by arrangement.
British microbrand producing vintage-inspired mechanical watches. Known for traditional watchmaking aesthetics and British design.
British independent watch brand producing tool watches and dress watches with modern design sensibilities and accessible pricing.
Founded 2015, based in Henley-on-Thames. Design-led British brand. Confirm if UK atelier visits are possible.
French independent based in Paris. Military and tool watch aesthetic with robust construction ethos. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watchmaker with heritage dating back to the 19th century. Produces mechanical watches using ETA and other Swiss movements at mid-range price points.
Zurich-based independent with artisan focus. Custom and bespoke dial work. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.
Founded in 2013 by Eric Mauron and Christophe Musy in Saint-Aubin, Fribourg. Known for patented nO-Ring gasket-free technology and strict 100% Swiss Crafted standard.
Italian microbrand known for vintage-inspired dive watches with robust designs and affordable pricing.
German independent watchmaker known for single-hand watches and minimalist dial designs. Produces mechanical watches with in-house movements.
Small independent Pforzheim-based brand. Confirm active status and visitability.
Danish microbrand focused on minimalist Scandinavian design and affordable quartz and mechanical watches.
French independent specialising in military-specification field and tool watches. Based in Paris. Known for robust construction and legible dials. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watch brand with historical roots, known for robust mechanical watches and vintage-inspired designs.
Historic Swiss watchmaker known for chronographs and precision movements, now producing limited-edition watches.
French micro-brand based in Paris. Known for colourful, accessible watches with Swiss movements and strong French lifestyle identity. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watch brand known for minimalist, Bauhaus-inspired designs and the iconic Swiss railway watch.
Swiss watch brand producing accessible mechanical and quartz watches.
French microbrand producing dive and sport watches with vintage-inspired aesthetics.
British microbrand known for quirky, unconventional watch designs with artistic dials and playful themes.
Paris-based French independent. Typography and graphic design-inspired dial aesthetic. Swiss-assembled. Direct-to-consumer. Visitability unconfirmed.
Nivada is a Swiss watch brand with a long history dating back to 1926. The company is known for diving watches and sport timepieces with vintage heritage.
Historic Grenchen brand, revived. Known for the Chronomaster diver. Movements by ETA/Sellita. Strong vintage-revival following. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand founded in 2016. Known for vintage-inspired diving watches and tool watches with Japanese and Swiss movements. Affordable price range, typically under $1,500.
Danish minimalist watch brand founded in 2017. Known for Scandinavian design-inspired dress watches with quartz and automatic movements. Entry-level pricing, typically under $300.
Swiss independent brand founded in 2017. Known for adventure watches combining Swiss heritage with modern design. Uses automatic movements; positioned in the mid-range market.
An Italian independent watch manufacturer known for minimalist and technical watch designs with Italian aesthetics.
Irish master watchmaker John McGonigle launched the Oilean brand in 2020, combining Swiss training with Irish cultural identity. Workshop established in Athlone, County Westmeath since 1999.
Founded in 2022 by Genevan watchmaker Olivier Gaud. All suppliers except strap makers are in French-speaking Switzerland. Workshop in Geneva's Rue des Rois.
Founded in 2014 by architect Miguel Morales Ribas. Spanish brand headquartered in Madrid using Swiss movements and German case/dial components.
Italian watchmaker producing traditional mechanical watches with in-house movements and restoration services.
An Italian independent watchmaker creating mechanical watches with traditional craftsmanship.
A Greek independent watch brand focused on minimalist and modern watch designs.

Swiss independent brand based in La Chaux-de-Fonds focused on retrograde and jumping hour complications with strong Art Deco and aviation aesthetic influences. Produces limited series not affiliated with any major group. No confirmed public visit or tour programme known.
A French watchmaker with heritage in watch production, though limited current information available.
Designed, assembled and tested in South Oxfordshire by founder Piers Berry since 2013. Movements sourced from Switzerland/Germany, final assembly UK. Small independent operation.
Spanish watch brand creating contemporary timepieces with minimalist design aesthetics.
A Lithuanian independent watchmaker creating mechanical watches with traditional craftsmanship and heritage.
British watch brand with roots in military timekeeping, producing robust mechanical watches for diving and tactical use.
Slovakian watchmaker with decades of history producing mechanical watches and military-style timepieces.
German design brand by Biegert & Funk headquartered in Schwaebisch Gmuend, Baden-Wuerttemberg. Produces distinctive word-based clocks and watches where the time is displayed as text.
Geneva independent known for the transparent sapphire mechanical watch movement. Visitability unconfirmed.
French independent based in Paris. Aviation and radial engine-inspired design language. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Norwegian watch brand associated with radium-related instrumentation.
Belgian independent inspired by endurance racing heritage (La Raidillon corner at Spa-Francorchamps). Swiss-assembled. Strong motorsport following. Visitability unconfirmed.
French independent based in La Rochelle. Specialises in high-performance diving and military watches with hybrid mechanical-quartz movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand known for watch designs inspired by dashboard instruments and automotive heritage, using ETA and Seagull movements.

Reservoir is a Paris-based French independent brand founded in 2018, distinguished by its industrial-gauge-inspired displays — retrograde fuel-gauge indicators, altimeter dials, and speedometer-style readouts applied to Swiss-movement timepieces. All design work originates from the Paris studio, making it the natural visit point. The brand has built a strong international following among design-conscious collectors. Visitability is by arrangement given the small team structure.

Belgian independent founded by Benoît Mintiens, known for the ROCS (Ressence Orbital Convex System) — a unique oil-filled rotating disc display replacing conventional hands. Design and brand HQ in Antwerp; movements produced in Switzerland. Visit the Antwerp studio by arrangement.
Swiss watchmaker founded in 1853, known for mechanical watches including military and sport models. Uses ETA and in-house movements, priced typically under $3,000.
Swiss watch brand founded in 1925, known for affordable mechanical watches with vintage designs. Uses ETA and Ronda movements, typically priced under $500.
Entirely English-made watches in a 16th-century building (The Mermaid) in Stamford. Family business, cases and dials manufactured locally. Highly personal visits possible.
British watch brand established in 1895, known for dress watches and chronographs with heritage positioning, using ETA and Ronda movements.
Swiss independent based in La Chaux-de-Fonds. Design-led with distinctive case shapes. Visitability unconfirmed.

Historic German watch brand from Ruhla, Thuringia — a town with deep watchmaking heritage dating back to the 19th century. Revived as a modern brand. Confirm current production status and visitability.
Italian luxury fashion house that produces watches alongside clothing and accessories. Known for elegant design and craftsmanship across multiple product categories.
Paris-based French independent. Salon-style atelier in Paris. Bespoke and small-series watches with handcrafted dials and cases. Visitability unconfirmed.
Founded in 2024 by Christophe Schaefer in Le Locle. Specialises in metiers d'art timepieces including enamel, engraving, marquetry, and micro-painting.
Small independent Glashütte brand. Confirm current production status and visitability.
British microbrand known for vintage-inspired military and tool watches with retro aesthetics and affordable price points.
British microbrand established 2008, known for affordable dive watches with bold colours and reliable quartz/automatic movements.
Italian brand known for sports watches and chronographs at mid-range pricing, active in motorsport and diving watch segments.
British watch brand established 1988, known for accessible dress and casual watches with quartz and automatic movements.
French independent based in Paris. Latin motto meaning 'Always and still.' Minimalist French design with Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Paris-based French independent, founded by Julien Tixier. Known for the 5303 diver and 8315 field watch. Swiss movements, strong French design identity. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand founded in 2012. Known for vintage-inspired automatic watches with in-house dial design and quality Japanese movements. Affordable luxury positioning.
Founded by Swiss-born engineer Martin Klocke, based in Meerbusch near Dusseldorf. Revives designs from the historic Swiss Enicar archive using German and Swiss components.
German independent manufacturer founded in 1961. Renowned for professional pilot and tool watches with in-house movement development and technical innovation.

Swedish watchmaker founded 1986 in Stockholm. Watchmakers conceive, assemble and fine-tune each watch at their Södermalm workshop (Ringvägen 50C, Stockholm). One of Scandinavia's most credible watch manufactures. Visits by arrangement.
Historic British watchmaker with heritage roots. Modern iteration produces affordable mechanical watches with classic styling.
Founded by Samuel Soleilhac (originally from Saint-Etienne, France). Brand and atelier are based in Geneva; collaborates with Swiss and French subcontractors.
French independent based in Paris. Space and aerospace-inspired design. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Independent British watchmaker founded in the 1990s. Known for handcrafted watches with technical innovation and bespoke commissioning.
Swiss dive watch specialist with heritage roots in professional diving equipment. Based in La Chaux-de-Fonds. Known for the 1521 diver. Visitability unconfirmed.
Italian independent watchmaker known for unique dial designs and small-batch production of automatic watches.
German independent brand founded in 2001. Known for affordable homage watches using ETA and Selita movements with high build quality.
Hamburg-based independent known for Bauhaus-influenced affordable watches. Swiss movements. Primarily e-commerce brand — no public atelier visits.
German watchmaker based in Hornberg with a history dating back to 1927. Known for Flieger-style pilot watches and tool watches with in-house movements and high-quality finishing.
Italian microbrand known for dive watches and sports watches with accessible pricing and Italian design sensibility.

Brighton-based independent brand founded by Daan Koper, known for cuisine-themed watch dials with genuine character and a strong community following. Grown rapidly from a one-man operation to thousands of watches per year. The D0ghouse in Brighton serves as the brand's atelier and creative hub. Fun, accessible brand — unusual and memorable visit for groups seeking something beyond traditional watch tourism.
British heritage brand with historical ties; modern independent operations producing dress and casual watches.
Swiss watchmaker producing military-style watches with practical designs.
Parisian independent with strong French identity. Known for colourful modular watch designs and strong social media presence. Swiss-assembled. Visitability unconfirmed.
British microbrand producing tool watches with industrial design aesthetics.
Düsseldorf-based independent. Confirm active status and visitability.
Italian microbrand producing vintage-inspired dive and field watches with attention to dial design and affordable pricing.
UK-based independent watchmaker known for vintage-inspired dive watches and tool watches. Uses ETA and Sellita movements with custom cases and dial designs.
Italian independent watchmaker focused on handcrafted mechanical watches with artisanal approach to design and movement finishing.
German watch brand specializing in tritium-illuminated tactical and professional watches. Known for extreme legibility and durability.
German-Italian watch brand revived as an independent brand, known for bauhaus-influenced design and field watches.
Swedish independent watch brand focused on minimalist Scandinavian design and accessible pricing. Known for slim profiles and contemporary aesthetics.
A German independent watch brand specializing in vintage-inspired mechanical watches and pilot-style timepieces.
German independent watchmaker with military heritage, known for chronographs and tool watches with German movement manufacturing.
Dutch independent brand founded in 2005, known for oversized steel sports watches and fashion-forward designs at accessible prices.
Italian independent based in Florence, founded by Italo Fontana. Known for oversized military-inspired cases and distinctive crown position. Visitability unconfirmed.
A British microbrand creating modern, minimalist watches with a focus on design and functionality.
Italian independent watchmaker focused on mechanical watches and artisanal craftsmanship.
German microbrand founded in 2015. Known for customizable modular watches with Sellita and Ronda movements, offering unique dial and case combinations at affordable prices.
Italian microbrand established in 2015. Specializes in minimalist dive watches with a focus on vintage aesthetics and Miyota quartz movements, based in Bologna.
A Norwegian watch brand focused on Scandinavian design and outdoor-oriented timepieces.
Belgian independent. Art-inspired dials and sculptural approach to watchmaking. Based in Brussels. Visitability unconfirmed.
French independent based in Paris. Named after explorer Vasco de Gama. Adventure and exploration watch aesthetic with Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Danish microbrand creating minimalist watches with Scandinavian design principles and accessible price points.
Venetian independent inspired by the heritage and architecture of Venice. Swiss-assembled with NH35/Miyota movements. Strong micro-brand following. Visitability unconfirmed.
Revival of the historic British military 'Dirty Dozen' WWII watch brand, relaunched 2016 by founder's great-grandson. Confirm current manufacture location and visitability.
Founded in 2010 by Vincent Plomb in Boncourt, Swiss Jura. Known for astronomical complications and 3D globe displays.
Swiss manufacturer known for Swiss Army watches combining tool functionality with watchmaking, using ETA and Ronda movements across affordable to mid-range price points.
Italian microbrand known for leather goods and watches combining craftsmanship traditions with accessible pricing.
French independent based in Paris. Design-led micro-brand with geometric and architectural dial language. Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss watchmaker founded in 1858. Historic brand known for mechanical alarm watches and cricket movements, now operating as an independent luxury manufacture.
British microbrand creating vintage-inspired dress and casual watches. Focus on accessible pricing and classic aesthetics.
London-based British independent known for the Sharkmaster dive watch. Swiss movements. Strong following in the UK and international micro-brand community. Visitability unconfirmed.
French microbrand revived from a 1960s heritage brand. Known for vintage-inspired dive watches and field watches with modern reliability.
French independent based in Paris. Inspired by the North Sea and Dutch-French coastal heritage. Maritime-themed design with Swiss movements. Visitability unconfirmed.
Swiss independent watchmaker creating pilot watches, dive watches, and chronographs. Known for mechanical reliability and vintage military-inspired designs.
Swiss microbrand known for bold, colorful dive watches with contemporary design. Offers quartz and automatic movements at accessible price points.
Historic French dive watch brand, founded 1904. Known for supplying the French Navy. Recently revived with modern reissues of the Grand Fonds diver. Based in Paris. Visitability unconfirmed.
